tip.cobblemontips.addons.text: Visit our Discord to find user-made addon packs for fakemon and other custom content!
tip.cobblemontips.ancient_poke_balls.tex: Tumblestones can be found in the ruins of the world. These funny little rocks can be used to craft replicas of Ancient Poké Balls.
tip.cobblemontips.apricorn_bone_meal.tex: You can use Bone Meal on Apricorn buds to advance their growth.
tip.cobblemontips.apricorn_seeds.text: Harvesting Apricorns sometimes drops Apricorn Seeds.
tip.cobblemontips.aprijuice.text: Give Aprijuice to your Pokémon to increase their ride stats! Different flavors provided by the Apricorns and Berries used increase different stats.
tip.cobblemontips.bait.text: Try attaching Berries to your Poké Rod when fishing. Each one has a different effect!
tip.cobblemontips.bed_sharing.text: Some small Pokémon will sleep on your bed with you if left outside of their Poké Ball when you use a bed.
tip.cobblemontips.beds.text: Your party will be fully healed if you sleep in a bed.
tip.cobblemontips.berries.text: There are many Berries you can find in the wild in different biomes.
tip.cobblemontips.berry_drops.text: Many Pokémon have a low chance to drop a Berry.
tip.cobblemontips.big_roots.text: Big Roots can be found in caves near the surface. They spread like Mushrooms, but on cave ceilings.
tip.cobblemontips.brushes.text: The past hides many secrets; use a Brush to uncover them in spots with suspicious-looking Sand and Gravel.
tip.cobblemontips.campfire_pot.text: Place a Campfire Pot on a Campfire to cook new foods that change their effects based on which extra seasonings you put in. Try experimenting with different seasonings!
tip.cobblemontips.candies.text: Exp. Candies can be made in a Campfire Pot, as well as be upgraded into larger Exp. Candies. Those can be cooked again into Candies that increase or decrease IVs!
tip.cobblemontips.cooking_medicines.text: Potions and other medicine items can be made in Campfire Pots. However, Brewing Stands are more resource efficient and can upgrade medicines even further.
tip.cobblemontips.cosmetic_slot.text: Pokémon have an additional hold item slot called a "cosmetic slot." Compatible items placed in this slot allow Pokémon to wear them cosmetically.
tip.cobblemontips.discord.text: Join the Cobblemon Discord to follow development progress, chat with the team, and get sneak peeks into upcoming features!
tip.cobblemontips.dispensers.text: Dispensers can use shears to automatically harvest fully grown Apricorns.
tip.cobblemontips.display_case.text: Try using a Display Case to show off your valuables!
tip.cobblemontips.dying_wool.text: You can dye the wool of Wooloo and Dubwool! Use a Water Bucket to wash the dyes off.
tip.cobblemontips.energy_roots.text: When Big Roots spread to a nearby block, sometimes they become an Energy Root instead.
tip.cobblemontips.ev_supplements.text: You can craft Feathers, cook Mochis, or brew Vitamins to give to your Pokémon to raise their stats through boosting EVs.
tip.cobblemontips.evo_stones.text: Evolution Stone ores are most common near the surface.
tip.cobblemontips.evolve.text: When a Pokémon is ready to evolve, you can find the "Evolve" button in its Summary screen.
tip.cobblemontips.feebas.text: Feebas are an elusive fish that only appear in specific areas... If you can't find any, try another fishing spot.
tip.cobblemontips.free_move.text: You can build, mine, or fight mobs while in Pokémon battles by switching to free-move mode.
tip.cobblemontips.friendship.text: Pokémon gain more Friendship when they are outside of their Poké Ball.
tip.cobblemontips.healers.text: Healers take some time to charge after being first placed down.
tip.cobblemontips.heart_wooper.text: It's said that some Wooper love sweets...
tip.cobblemontips.hearty_grains.text: Hearty Grains grow in shallow water in many temperate or wet climates. They can be made into many foods that Pokémon can eat.
tip.cobblemontips.hold_items.text: Pokémon can be given an item to hold with "Shift + Right-Click".
tip.cobblemontips.honey_slathering.text: If you slather a Honey Bottle on a Saccharine Log, the combined scent is so sweet it could probably lure out even the most hidden Pokémon...
tip.cobblemontips.hybrids.text: Try planting different Berries next to each other. You might get something new!
tip.cobblemontips.magikarp_patterns.text: Rarely, Magikarp with unusual patterns are caught by anglers. Will you be able to lure them out of hiding?
tip.cobblemontips.medicinal_brew.text: Medicinal Brew is the base for brewing refined Pokémon healing items like Potions.
tip.cobblemontips.medicinal_leeks.text: Medicinal Leeks are found commonly in shallow water. You can cook them or brew them into Medicinal Brew.
tip.cobblemontips.mints.text: Mints are elusive plants known to favor high elevations.
tip.cobblemontips.mulch.text: You can put Mulch on Berry trees to increase their growth speed or yields. Different Berries prefer certain kinds of Mulch.
tip.cobblemontips.nero_whiscash.text: There are stories that some Whiscash grow to monsterous sizes... You'd surely have to be a master angler to catch one.
tip.cobblemontips.pastures.text: Do you want to let your Pokémon roam around outside? Craft a Pasture and let them loose!
tip.cobblemontips.pep_up_flowers.text: Revival Herbs have two harvestable stages. Once fully grown, they'll eventually grow a Pep-Up Flower if left long enough.
tip.cobblemontips.poke_bait.text: A Campfire Pot lets you combine things you could normally use as fishing bait to make Poké Bait. Wild Pokémon may find these even tastier!
tip.cobblemontips.poke_balls.text: Basic Poké Balls come in all 7 Apricorn colors!
tip.cobblemontips.poke_rod_templates.tex: Poké Rod Templates can be fished up as treasure, found in secret structures, or obtained from trading with Villagers of the Fisherman profession.
tip.cobblemontips.poke_rods.text: Enchanting a Poké Rod will increase your chances of fishing up rare Pokémon.
tip.cobblemontips.poke_snacks.text: Having a hard time hunting down a specific Pokémon? Try luring them out with their favorite kinds of Poké Snacks. Pokémon can't resist them!
tip.cobblemontips.pokedex.text: Craft a Pokédex to view Pokémon you've seen or caught.
tip.cobblemontips.pokemon_bone_meal.text: If plants grow faster when you use Bone Meal on them, what happens when you use it on plant Pokémon?
tip.cobblemontips.pokemon_brushing.text: Some Pokémon have a bit of a shedding problem. Have you tried brushing yours lately?
tip.cobblemontips.ponigiri.text: Ponigiri can be made with many different savory fillings that further satisfy one's hunger. But would it be strange to try a sweeter filling...?
tip.cobblemontips.regen.text: Pokémon slowly regain HP, cure statuses, and recover from fainting over time.
tip.cobblemontips.regional_foods.text: It's been said the unique taste of delicacies from distant regions makes one feel like they're actually there.
tip.cobblemontips.relearn.text: Pokémon can change and relearn moves at any time with the arrows to the right of the move slot.
tip.cobblemontips.relic_coins.text: Relic Coins can be found across the world. For one reason or another, Gimmighoul seem to really like them.
tip.cobblemontips.remedies.text: Big Roots can be crafted into Remedies, which restore a Pokémon's HP at the cost of slightly lowering friendship.
tip.cobblemontips.removing_mulch.text: Mulches can be removed with a Shovel.
tip.cobblemontips.restoration_tank.text: Anything organic can be used to fuel a Restoration Tank, from Leaves and Steak to Spider Eyes and Turtle Eggs.
tip.cobblemontips.resurrection_machine.t: A Resurrection Machine is actually made up of three separate blocks; the Data Monitor, Fossil Compartment, and Restoration Tank.
tip.cobblemontips.revival_herbs.text: Revival Herbs are rare plants found in Lush Caves underground. Perhaps their revival properties could be useful for more than reviving fainted Pokémon...
tip.cobblemontips.ride_styles.text: Rideable Pokémon have separate stats for Land, Air, and Water movement. But did you know different species have different ride styles within these categories?
tip.cobblemontips.running.text: You have to run from battles by actually running away!
tip.cobblemontips.saccharine_trees.text: Saccharine Trees are peculiar blue trees whose sweet honey-like scent attracts certain Pokémon. Bees seem to be particularly fond of them.
tip.cobblemontips.scanning.text: A Pokédex can be used to scan Pokémon from afar to record basic information about them.
tip.cobblemontips.servers.text: There aren't any official Cobblemon servers, but you can find servers to play on by visiting our website or Discord and checking the server listings.
tip.cobblemontips.shearing.text: Try shearing Wooloo or milking Miltank!
tip.cobblemontips.shiny_pokemon.text: Pokémon have a 1/8,192 chance to have a different coloration.
tip.cobblemontips.shiny_sounds.text: Keep an ear out for twinkling sounds. If you hear one, something special may be nearby!
tip.cobblemontips.shoulder.text: Some small Pokémon can ride on your shoulder with "Shift + Right-Click."
tip.cobblemontips.sinister_tea.text: When added to stew, different plants can grant all kinds of suspicious effects... so what sinister brew could you make if you combined them into tea?
tip.cobblemontips.sleeping_pokemon.text: Some Pokémon can be found sleeping. Sneak up on them for an advantage in battle!
tip.cobblemontips.surprise_mulch.text: Try putting Surprise Mulch on a Revival Herb plant. Who knows what you'll get?
tip.cobblemontips.trading.text: You can interact with other players to request a battle or trade.
tip.cobblemontips.tumblestones.text: Tumblestones seem to grow in size near sources of geological heat. Try placing them down near lava or magma.
tip.cobblemontips.white_mints.text: White Mint plants may not seem very useful, but try growing some and you may get a surprise.
tip.cobblemontips.wiki.text: Not sure where to find something? You can find detailed information about the mod on the Cobblemon Wiki!
